name: Twilio API
what_it_does: Twilio REST API — cloud communications platform enabling agents to programmatically send SMS/MMS, make and receive voice calls, manage phone numbers, and build communication workflows via a simple REST interface with webhooks for inbound messages and calls.
use_cases: Agents sending SMS notifications — POST to /Messages to send OTP codes, appointment reminders, or system alerts to users' phone numbers
Voice call automation — agents initiating outbound calls with pre-built TwiML (Twilio Markup Language) for IVR flows, announcements, or call routing
Inbound message handling — agents processing webhook callbacks when users reply to SMS, parsing keywords, and triggering appropriate workflows
Phone number provisioning — agents searching for and purchasing available phone numbers via API for new customer accounts or campaigns
Conversation tracking — agents using Twilio Conversations API to manage multi-channel message threads (SMS + WhatsApp + chat) with participant state
not_for: High-volume marketing campaigns at minimal cost — Twilio's per-message pricing becomes expensive at scale; use dedicated bulk SMS providers or Twilio's marketing products
WhatsApp business messaging without Twilio sandbox approval — WhatsApp via Twilio requires Meta Business approval which takes weeks; use Meta Cloud API for direct access
Real-time sub-100ms voice processing — Twilio Media Streams work but adding AI processing introduces latency; purpose-built voice AI platforms (Vapi, Bland) are better for AI voice agents
best_when: You need reliable SMS/voice communications with simple REST API integration, good webhook support, and broad carrier coverage for notification and verification workflows.
avoid_when: You need ultra-high-volume SMS at lowest cost, real-time AI voice conversations, or WhatsApp messaging without going through Twilio's approval process.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth with Account SID (username) and Auth Token (password). API Keys can be created as scoped credentials (safer than Auth Token). Webhook requests signed with X-Twilio-Signature header (HMAC-SHA1) for request validation. Separate credentials per sub-account for multi-tenant isolation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Trial account: $15.50 credit, can only send to verified numbers
pricing.paid_tiers: SMS: $0.0079/message (US outbound); phone numbers: $1.15/month (US local); voice: $0.014/min; pricing varies by country
pricing.notes: Per-message and per-minute pricing. Geographic pricing varies significantly. Volume discounts available. Messaging Services (toll-free, A2P 10DLC) have different pricing. Trial account restricted to verified phone numbers only.

performance.rate_limits: Default: 1 message/second per phone number; Messaging Services: up to 1000/s with message queuing; API requests: 100/s

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent documentation with code examples in 7 languages. Error responses include numeric error codes (21211 = invalid To number, 21610 = unsubscribed recipient) with links to error documentation. SDK exceptions map to HTTP errors cleanly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message sending is not idempotent — same call creates duplicate messages. Agents must implement application-level deduplication. Webhook status callbacks (sent/delivered/failed) are idempotent by MessageSid. Phone number purchases idempotent by number.
known_agent_gotchas: Trial accounts can only send to verified phone numbers — agents in dev/test must verify recipient numbers in Twilio console or upgrade to a paid account
A2P 10DLC registration required for US SMS campaigns — agents sending marketing SMS without registration face carrier filtering; registration takes days to weeks to approve
Message queuing for high-throughput requires Messaging Services — sending via direct phone number at >1 msg/s causes queuing delays; use Messaging Services for throughput
Webhook validation using X-Twilio-Signature is critical for security — agents accepting Twilio webhooks without signature validation are vulnerable to spoofed requests
Opt-out keywords (STOP, UNSUBSCRIBE) are automatically processed by Twilio and result in 21610 errors on future sends — agents must handle these gracefully and maintain suppression lists

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA available. TLS enforced. Webhook signature validation (HMAC-SHA1). API Key scoping available (safer than Auth Token). Sub-accounts for tenant isolation. Auth Token rotation supported. GDPR compliant with EU data residency.
